description: Stub-fill additions from the 2026-05-04 cross-area pass that promoted four stub notes to draft: interpretability/lens-techniques, interpretability/probing, evaluation/knowledge-benchmarks, alignment/safety-evaluation. Adds new paper entries (chuang2023-dola, alain-bengio-2017, hewitt2019-structural-probe, belinkov2022-probing-survey, wei2023-jailbroken, nostalgebraist2020-logit-lens) and updates excerpts_file / notes_referenced_by for existing entries that now have grounded excerpts (belrose2023-tuned-lens, marks-tegmark-2023-truth, hendrycks2021-mmlu, mmlu-redux-2024, harmbench2024, chao2024-jailbreakbench, russinovich2024-crescendo).

new_papers.title: DoLa: Decoding by Contrasting Layers Improves Factuality in Large Language Models
new_papers.authors: Chuang, Xie, Luo, Kim, Glass, He
new_papers.summary: Decoding-time application of the logit-lens framework: contrast logits at the final layer against logits at an adaptively-chosen earlier layer (chosen per-token by JS divergence) to amplify factual signal and suppress hallucination. Improves TruthfulQA and FACTOR scores on LLaMA-7B/13B/33B with negligible inference overhead. Note: PDF not yet downloaded; excerpts from arXiv abstract pending grounding via WebSearch.

new_papers.title: Understanding intermediate layers using linear classifier probes
new_papers.summary: Foundational paper for the linear-classifier-probe methodology. Introduces the probe definition: a linear classifier trained on frozen network activations to predict a property, with the network's parameters held fixed. Empirical finding: linear separability of features increases monotonically with depth on Inception v3 / ResNet-50. The methodology generalizes to NLP probing in subsequent work. PDF not yet downloaded.

new_papers.title: A Structural Probe for Finding Syntax in Word Representations
new_papers.summary: Generalizes linear probing from binary classification to geometric / structural probing of higher-arity properties. Defines a 'structural probe' that learns a linear transform B such that squared-L2 distance in B-space approximates parse-tree distance, and a depth probe such that squared-L2 norm approximates parse-tree depth. Finds such transforms exist for ELMo and BERT but not for randomly-initialized baselines. PDF not yet downloaded.

new_papers.title: Probing Classifiers: Promises, Shortcomings, and Advances
new_papers.summary: Methodology survey that consolidates the correlational critique of probing and reviews causal alternatives. Three confounders documented: probe expressivity (powerful probe learns property from low-information features), spurious correlation (probe reads correlated feature, not target), vestigial information (model represents but doesn't use). Surveys causal probing methods (Amnesic, causal mediation, interchange interventions). PDF not yet downloaded.

new_papers.title: Jailbroken: How Does LLM Safety Training Fail?
new_papers.summary: Articulates the two-failure-mode framework for jailbreaks: (1) competing objectives (capability vs. safety pull in different directions), (2) mismatched generalization (safety training fails to generalize to domains where capability exists, e.g., Base64 / role-play). Empirically demonstrates that attacks designed against either failure mode succeed on every prompt in red-team evaluation sets for GPT-4 and Claude v1.3. Recommends 'safety-capability parity' as the policy upshot. PDF not yet downloaded.

new_papers.title: interpreting GPT: the logit lens
new_papers.summary: The original LessWrong post introducing the logit lens technique on GPT-2. Tier B/C forum source — cited as the discovery-path provenance for the technique, never as the sole citation for hard claims. The peer-reviewed treatment of the underlying methodology is `belrose2023-tuned-lens`.
